Curriculum vitae



Last Name: REYMOND

First Name: Alexandre

Date of Birth: 29 September 1964

Place of Birth: Vevey, Vaud, Switzerland

Origin: Vaulion, Vaud, Switzerland

Familial Status: married, father of a boy born the 24th December 1994


Work Address: Dept. of Mol. Biology, Massachusetts General Hospital and
Dept. of Genetics, Harvard Medical School
50 Blossom Street
Boston, MA 02114, USA
phone: (617) 726-5956
fax: (617) 726-6893
E-mail: reymond@frodo.mgh.harvard.edu

1984/ Certificat de MaturitÄ fÄdÄrale Type C

Honors and Awards:
1 to 12.1993/ Bourse de Chailly long term Fellowship for
the fourth year of PhD studies

5 to 6.1994/ Ciba-Geigy-JubilŖums-Stiftung short term

Publications:
Marks, J., C.Fankhauser, A.Reymond, and V.Simanis
(1992), Cytoskeletal and DNA structure abnormalities result
from bypass of requirement for the cdc10 start gene in the
fission yeast Schizosaccharomyces pombe.
J.Cell Sci. 101: 517-52.

Publications:
Reymond, A., S.Schmidt, and V.Simanis (1992), Mutations (continue)
in the cdc10 start gene of Schizosaccharomyces pombe
implicate the homologous region of cdc10 and SWI6 as
important for p85 function.
Mol. Gen. Genet. 234: 449-456

Fankhauser, C., J.Marks, A.Reymond, and V.Simanis
(1993), The S.pombe cdc16 gene is required both for
maintenance of p34 kinase activity and regulation of
septum formation: a link between mitosis and cytokinesis?
EMBO J. 12: 2697-2704

Reymond, A., J.Marks, and V.Simanis (1993), The Activity
of the S.pombe DSC1-like factor is cell cycle regulated and
dependent upon the activity of p34.
EMBO J. 12: 4325-4334.

Reymond, A., and V.Simanis (1993), Domains of p85
required for function of the fission yeast DSC1 factor.
Nucleic Acids Res. 21:3615-3621

Reymond, A. (1993), The Schizosaccharomyces pombe
DSC1 complex: cell cycle regulation and functional domains
of p85 . ThÅse de Doctorat de l'UniversitÄ de Lausanne
et de l'ISREC

Fankhauser, C., A.Reymond, L.Cerutti, S.Utzig,
Hofmann, K. and V.Simanis (1995), The
Schizosaccharomyces pombe cdc15 gene is required for F-
actin reorganisation at mitosis: Checkpoint bypass by ectopic
expression and regulation by phosphorylation.
Cell 82: 435-444

Reymond, A. and R.Brent (1995), p16 proteins from
melanoma-prone families are deficient in binding to cdk4,
Oncogene 11, pp 1173-1178.


Meetings:
1991/ Poster presented at the Warwick University, Fission
yeast Cell Cycle Meeting


1992/ Poster presented at the EMBL, Heidelberg,
Oncogenes and Growth Control Meeting

1993/ Poster presented at the CSH Laboratory, NY, Yeast

1994/ Cancer and Development, Boston, MA

1995/ Invited speaker at the Aussois, France, "The Cell
Division Cycle" Meeting

1995/ Invited speaker at the Iles des Embiez, Marseilles,
France, 5th International Workshop on the Identification of
transcribed Sequences Meeting.

1996/ Cancer and Cell Cycle, Lausanne, Switzerland

Hobbies/Outdoors Activities:
Birdwatching, Mammalogy, Hiking, Skiing

Publications:
Reymond, A. and O.Zuchuat (1995), Perch Fidelity of (hobbies related)
Cormorants Phalacrocorax carbo outside the breeding
season, Ardea 83, pp 281-284.

Reymond, A. and O.Zuchuat (1995), Axial Migration routes
in Cormorants Phalacrocorax carbo passing through or
wintering in Switzerland, Ardea 83, pp 275-280.
